description_long: This research institute consists of several interconnected rectangular structures arranged on a flat, dark base, resembling a modern academic or laboratory complex. The buildings feature flat roofs, some with visible rooftop installations or skylights, and are primarily composed of light gray and blue-tinted surfaces, suggesting the use of concrete, glass, and metal. The complex is surrounded by evenly spaced streetlights, indicating a well-lit exterior environment suitable for pedestrian and vehicular access. The main structures vary in height and footprint, with the central building appearing slightly larger and more prominent. The overall design is geometric and functional, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation. The layout suggests multiple entry points and internal courtyards or walkways connecting the different wings.
description: A modern, multi-wing research institute with flat roofs, light gray and blue facades, and a geometric, interconnected layout on a dark base with surrounding streetlights.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with several rectangular wings and rooftop details, flanked by streetlights.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the complex and the staggered arrangement of the building wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades and additional rooftop features, with the building wings clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the length of the complex and the alignment of the streetlights along the perimeter.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is not distinctly visible, but the design suggests a wide, accessible entrance likely integrated into the central building, possibly with glass doors and a flat approach for accessibility. No steps or decorative elements are clearly visible.
